title: Subsampling Ethanol Preservative from Zooplankton Museum Collections for DNA Extractions

This protocol is used to sample DNA from archived samples preserved in ethanol, without having to subsample or split the actual zooplankton biomass.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eddak","text":"
Spatial coverage and environments of relevance
As part of the Hakai Institute Ocean Observing Program, biomolecular samples have been collected weekly, from 0 to near bottom (260 m), to genetically characterize plankton communities in the Northern Salish Sea since 2015, developing a climatology from which we can begin uncover the physical, chemical and biological drivers of community and functional change in the dynamic coastal waters of coastal British Columbia. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":1,"length":46},{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":46}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dcp4f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d6s17","text":"This protocol has been used as an alternative source of genetic information, when it is not practical to remove biomass from the zooplankton samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c217j","text":"
Personnel Required
 1 Technician

before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8i3ar","text":"Read background information, MIOP and BePOP-OBON information under the \"Guidelines\" tab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: To re suspend DNA in the zooplankton sample, invert the sample jar three times.
Use a serological pipette to remove 50 ml of the ethanol preservative, and transfer to a 50 mL falcon tube.
Use a syringe to push the ethanol through a Sterivex Filter (0.2 um) Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #SVGPL10RC .
Seal the outflow of the filter with parafilm.
Seal the inflow opening with parafilm.
Label filter units and store them at -80°C for downstream DNA extraction.
Prepare Sucrose Lysis Buffer (SLB): Sucrose lysis buffer by Andreas Novotny
Follow the same extraction procedures as for Environmental DNA: DNA Extraction from 0.22µm Sterivex Filters - Phenol-Chloroform by Andreas Novotny
Alternative extraction method: DNA Extraction from 0.22µm Sterivex Filters - Qiagen Blood and Tissue Kit by Andreas Novotny
creator: Andreas Novotny
